---
title: "LRP86: Period-resolved dose-response — intervention dose -> letter sounds (L)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP86 is the [#104](https://github.com/dseinternational/language-reading-predictors/issues/104)
Phase-3 **generalisation of [LRP77](../lrp77/) from word reading to letter-sound
knowledge** ($L$, YARC-LSK, $N = 32$): does the small dose effect found for word
reading extend to a decoding skill? It is the identical period-resolved
dose-response model — Beta-Binomial conditional change on $L_{\text{post}} \mid
L_{\text{pre}}$, per-period dose (`attend`) with partial-pooled period slopes,
`attend_cumul` dose-stage control, $\{G, A, L_{\text{pre}}\}$ adjustment (DAG v5:
$G$ the sole dose confounder, $L_{\text{pre}}$ the RTM baseline, age a precision
covariate), subject random intercept — with the outcome swapped to $L$. All gain
rows are kept, including the zero-dose anchor.

::: {.callout-note title="Expectations"}
The Phase-1 GB diagnostic showed dose importance for letter-sound *gains* sat
near the noise floor — much lower than for word reading. So this is a
generalisation **check** likely to return a **weak or null** dose slope: a useful
"dose helps word reading specifically, not decoding broadly" result rather than
an expected positive finding.
:::

Read the credible **pooled** slope from the comparator [LRP86base](../lrp86base/)
(the period-varying model's *overall* slope has an inflated CI from the
partial-pooling hyper-prior); the LRP86-vs-LRP86base PSIS-LOO answers whether the
$L$ dose–gain slope varies by period. See the LRP77 report for the full
parameterisation, the $G = 1$ = waitlist coding, and the
adjusted-association / weak-effect caveats.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Dose slope (the estimand)

```{python}
# | echo: false
dose = pd.read_csv(_here / "dose_slope_summary.csv")
dose
```

`dose_overall` is the pooled (hyper-mean) slope; `dose_period1..3` the
partial-pooled per-period slopes; `sigma_dose_between_period` their spread. All on
the logit scale per 1 SD of per-period dose; `p_pos` is $P(\text{slope} > 0)$.
Compare with the word-reading slopes in the LRP77 report.

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
